message:
test(engine): expand IFC flow-qualified declassification authorization edge case coverage

Add targeted tests that exercise the information-flow-control
authorization assessment logic for mismatched and cross-label
scenarios:

- ifc_artifacts_edge_cases: New test
  sealed_sink_ignores_non_matching_flow_authorization_refs verifies
  that an authorization ref qualifying a different (label, clearance)
  pair does not accidentally satisfy a Secret→SealedSink flow check.
  The envelope is authorized (labels and clearances match), but the
  flow itself remains blocked because the declassification ref
  targets top_secret, not secret.

- ifc_artifacts_integration: Fix the existing
  flow_envelope_secret_to_sealed_sink_requires_concrete_obligation
  test to use the flow-qualified authorization ref format
  ("sealed_sink:secret:obl-secret-sealed") introduced in the
  flow-qualified declassification feature. Add new test
  flow_envelope_top_secret_to_sealed_sink_ignores_secret_only_authorization_refs
  verifying that a secret-scoped authorization ref does not grant
  declassification for a TopSecret source — the assessment must
  surface a DeclassificationObligationRequired advisory instead.

These tests close gaps in the authorization matrix where label/clearance
mismatches could have been silently accepted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
